mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of instances when unemployment rises above the threshold over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates broad-based domestic demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in consumption and housing fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a Rank IC above 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains do not translate to positive 5-day returns in recovery periods with a win rate above 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation months consistently precede positive 20-day equity returns without subsequent policy intervention.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, supporting corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth combined with falling unemployment fails to predict positive quarterly returns with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real gross domestic product reflects sustained economic growth and higher corporate revenues, directly driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative industrial production changes are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 60% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.
